meta.note: 完全参照 AIGC-ZEN-VOID 道具面板模板，并收敛到当前仓库 projects/aigc/<项目名>/4-设计 runtime。

prompt_payload.layout_generation_prompt: Create one cinematic PROP_ATMOSPHERIC_DOSSIER on a 4096x2304 widescreen canvas (16:9). Use a weathered charcoal base (#171615) with faint analog grain and restrained technical grid (#2B2826). Keep a fixed three-column layout LEFT 15% | CENTER 50% | RIGHT 35%, preserve readability for production review, and allow subdued world-coherent pedestal or threshold context behind the prop without turning the sheet into a poster.

modules.label: MATERIAL PALETTE (4x3)
modules.description: Show 12 swatches for core material families, roughness variation, and wear state references.

modules.label: COLOR PALETTE (2x3)
modules.description: Show primary/secondary/accent color chips with tonal hierarchy suitable for the same prop identity.

modules.label: HERO OBJECT TRIPTYCH
modules.description: Render one hero frontal or three-quarter view, one secondary angle proving scale or tension direction, and one silhouette verification view. Avoid sterile front/side/back turnaround logic when the prop is symbolic, organic, or ritual.

modules.label: DETAIL CLOSE-UPS (2x3)
modules.description: Render six close-up cells for craftsmanship joints, surface wear, and narrative marks.

modules.label: WORLD CONTEXT (2 frames)
modules.description: Render two context frames showing pedestal placement, ritual threshold, environmental use, or spatial tension while keeping identical prop morphology. No human hands, no character takeover.

mandatory_rules: Output must remain a three-column prop dossier rather than a single product poster or a sterile industrial turnaround board.
The upstream prop design prompt is the primary source of truth; layout instructions organize the sheet but must not override prop identity, scale, material facts, era, style, or narrative function.
Reference images, when provided, are continuity anchors only; do not copy unrelated composition, scale, surface noise, or accidental artifacts from references.
All modules must inherit the upstream design direction and explicit anti-drift guardrails; composition can elaborate them but must not reinterpret core prop identity.
All modules must depict the same prop identity with stable silhouette, scale, and material logic.

Must reserve one fixed top-left identity badge area using matched roster text `<PROP_ID>+<PROP_NAME>`; if exact text rendering is unreliable, preserve the badge area and short ID cleanly for post-production text overlay.
Part 3 is layout-only composition; do not redesign narrative facts or core prop identity inherited from `5-设计/道具/2-设计`.
No people, no hands, no holding, and no human figure should dominate any context frame.
Include clear scale logic through non-human cues such as surface grain, pedestal proportion, table edge, tile size, measurement ticks, or environmental contact shadow.
Show functional affordance: how the object is opened, worn, placed, activated, damaged, sealed, or ritually used, while keeping the prop morphology unchanged.
Close-up cells must preserve the same material aging and construction logic as the hero object; do not invent new ornaments or extra parts in detail views.
The sheet describes one prop only; do not multiply it into a collection, emblem set, product lineup, or decorative cluster unless the upstream prop is explicitly a set.
For symbolic or mythic props, prefer threshold, pedestal, altar, water-edge, or abyss-edge context over front/side/back technical rotation.

Do not drift into luxury product ad, neutral museum packshot, enlarged emblem abstraction, multi-object cluster, or web-like substitute morphology unless the upstream prop contract explicitly demands it.
Context frames exist only to prove world logic, era anchoring, and usage tension; they must not mutate the prop into a different object class.
Micro-scale or symbolic props must still read as literal world objects first and abstract symbols second.
